# Map & de-friction the customer journey (signposts, risk-removal, friction)

Setup: Maya is mid-exit on her home-goods brand. Her flagship organic-cotton bedding line
(ASIN B0HOM0003 — "Nesting Upgrader," US women 30–50, AOV $140, repeat 31%) converts fine at the
top but bleeds in the middle: PDP traffic is healthy, add-to-cart-to-purchase is soft, and post-purchase
loyalty isn't compounding. For a valuation lift she wants the *whole journey* mapped to its emotional
signposts and a concrete risk-removal / friction-fix list a buyer can see is already actionable — not a theory deck.

---

**Maya (User):** B0HOM0003, organic-cotton bedding, flagship, mid-sale. Funnel's leaking in the middle — traffic's fine, ATC-to-purchase is soft, repeat's stuck at 31%. I want the full customer journey mapped to the emotional signposts, plus a real risk-removal + friction list. Map's the easy part — what I actually need is the fix list a buyer can see is shovel-ready. No theory deck. Use my data. Just build it.

**Coach:** On it — five-stage map keyed to its signpost emotion, then a stage-by-stage risk + friction fix list, exported as a doc. One framing up front, because it's the load-bearing claim of the whole chapter: customers never buy randomly — every move between stages is fueled by a specific emotional signpost, so a stage that leaks is a stage whose signpost emotion isn't being addressed. Your soft middle is a Consideration→Decision signpost gap, not a traffic problem. I'll pull your funnel data into structured context first so the map runs against your numbers, not a template.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/01-the-five-stages-and-signposts/00-the-five-stage-funnel.md⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation — input {message: "<Maya's B0HOM0003 funnel: healthy PDP traffic, soft ATC→purchase, repeat 31%, exit-prep, valuation lift>", avatar_context: "av_B0HOM0003 'Nesting Upgrader', US women 30–50, AOV $140, repeat 31%, organic-cotton bedding"}⟧
→ extracted_context: { product: "organic-cotton bedding (B0HOM0003)", avatar: "Nesting Upgrader — post-move/post-reno, wants the bedroom to feel finished/calm/theirs; skeptical of vague 'organic'", funnel_signal: "top healthy, middle soft (ATC→purchase), retention flat at 31%", dominant_fears: ["wrong fit/feel can't tell softness online","is the 'organic' real or greenwashed","wasted spend at $140 AOV"], context_window: "spikes during move-in / renovation completion", goal: "valuation lift via shovel-ready journey fixes" }

**Maya:** Right — the middle. And don't generic-advice me on "build trust." I know trust is the lever. Tell me *where* on the journey and *with what move*.

**Coach:** That's exactly what the signpost map pins down — the move is matched to the *feeling* the stage owns, not a blanket "trust" verb. Stage by stage, with your data: Awareness owns **curiosity/frustration**, Consideration owns **hope/anticipation**, Decision owns **trust/relief** (the moment they anticipate the "Shopper High"), Retention owns **satisfaction/validation**, Advocacy owns **pride/belonging**. Your leak sits on Decision's signpost — trust/relief is unaddressed at the moment of choice, which is precisely where a $140 organic claim has to convert skepticism into relief. Map drafted below; building the fix list against it now.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/01-the-five-stages-and-signposts/02-the-signposts-per-stage.md⟧

**Maya:** Good. Now the fix list. Two things have to be in it or it's useless: the risk-removal devices that actually disarm *my* category's fear, and the friction that's killing ATC. Pick the right ones — don't dump a menu on me.

**Coach:** Agreed — picked, not dumped. The selection rule from the playbook: identify the dominant fear in your category, then pick the risk-removal category that disarms *that* fear. Yours is fit/feel anxiety ("can't tell softness online") stacked on authenticity doubt ("is the organic real") — so try-before-you-buy / generous returns disarm the fit fear, and a performance/satisfaction guarantee plus visible proof disarms the authenticity fear. Both stated plainly at Decision, where trust/relief lives. And underneath all of it is the real adoption barrier: anxiety about changing from the bedding they already own — Moesta's "habit of the present" — so the job isn't only to sell softness, it's to make switching feel like a small, safe, familiar step.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/02-removing-anxiety-and-risk/01-risk-removal-strategies.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/02-removing-anxiety-and-risk/00-overcoming-anxiety-habit-of-the-present.md⟧

**Maya:** The ATC drop specifically. What friction, what fix.

**Coach:** Walk-the-journey diagnostic, matched to the book's friction→solution patterns. Your ATC-to-purchase soft spot maps to two named frictions: checkout effort (long/manual checkout → cart abandonment; fix = eliminate steps / autofill-Apple Pay, the Amazon one-click + Google-autofill pattern) and commitment fear at the moment of purchase (fix = the Netflix-style easy-exit / Zappos free-returns reassurance so the click feels reversible). The anticipate-barriers pass adds the one your category always hits: buying fabric you can't touch — the Warby-Parker home-try-on / Casper risk-free-trial pattern, adapted to bedding as a sleep-on-it trial. Every friction removed is conversion recovered. Exporting the map + fix list as a doc now.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/03-timing-and-friction/03-addressing-friction-points.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/01-customer-journey/03-timing-and-friction/04-anticipating-barriers.md⟧

⟦tool: export_strategy_doc — input {avatar_id: "av_B0HOM0003", brand_context: "<extracted_context>", section: "customer_journey_map", format: "doc"}⟧
→ strategy doc generated: 5-stage signpost map (stage · signpost emotion · current asset · gap) + risk-removal/friction fix list (fear → device → journey stage → book pattern); idempotent per avatar snapshot.
⟦iv-os: log_asset — journey-map doc linked to B0HOM0003⟧ *(mocked: no canon/asset/review/valuation data invented by coach)*

**Coach:** Map + fix list below. The map shows *where* each signpost emotion is or isn't being served; the fix list is shovel-ready — each row is a fear, the device that disarms it, the stage it belongs on, and the proven pattern it copies. Your leak is concentrated at Decision (trust/relief) and the ATC checkout/commitment friction, so the top three rows are the valuation-relevant ones.

### Artifact produced

**Customer Journey Map — B0HOM0003 (organic-cotton bedding) — exit-ready DRAFT, edit-ready**

**Part 1 — Five-Stage Signpost Map** *(each stage's signpost = the emotion that moves the customer to the next stage)*

| Stage | Emotional Signpost | What it needs | Your current state (from your data) | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Awareness** | Curiosity / frustration | Recognize the *need* (a bedroom that finally feels finished/calm), not sell a generic "organic bedding solution" | PDP traffic healthy — top of funnel works | Minor — connect on the nesting need, not the product spec |
| **Consideration** | Hope / anticipation | Help her picture the *outcome* (real-people imagery, warm tone) so she aspires to the finished room | Browsing happens; softness/authenticity unproven | The hope is there but unconverted — proof of feel + real "organic" is thin |
| **Decision** | **Trust / relief** (+ anticipating the "Shopper High") | Reassure at the moment of choice — testimonials + a clear, plainly-stated guarantee so the choice feels confident | **Soft ATC→purchase — the leak** | **Primary gap: trust/relief unaddressed at $140; skepticism not converted to relief** |
| **Retention** | Satisfaction / validation | Make her feel she chose right — a personalized post-purchase touch (the styling-tips thank-you pattern) | Repeat flat at 31% | Validation moment is missing — no reinforcement that the choice was right |
| **Advocacy** | Pride / belonging | Make her *proud* to share (unprompted reviews/unboxing because she belongs, not because asked) | No structured advocacy loop | Downstream of Retention — fix Retention first |

**Part 2 — Risk-Removal & Friction Fix List** *(fear → device → stage → proven book pattern)*

| # | Fear / friction (her category) | Disarming move (the device) | Journey stage | Pattern copied (book example) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Fit/feel anxiety** — "I can't tell if it's actually soft from a screen" | Sleep-on-it risk-free trial / try-before-you-buy + free, easy returns — make the click reversible | Decision | Try-before-you-buy (Casper 100-night trial) + money-back/returns (Zappos free 365-day returns) — risk-removal categories matched to fit anxiety |
| 2 | **Authenticity doubt** — "is the 'organic' real or greenwashed?" | Satisfaction/performance guarantee stated plainly *with* the certification shown at the point of choice | Decision | Satisfaction-or-money-back (Chewy 100% guarantee) — risk-removal disarms the bad-decision fear; deployed *with* visible proof |
| 3 | **Checkout effort** — manual entry / extra steps soften ATC→purchase | Eliminate steps: one-click + autofill / Apple Pay so buying is effortless | Decision (moment of purchase) | E-commerce checkout (Amazon one-click) + online forms (Google autofill / Apple Pay) — friction→solution |
| 4 | **Commitment fear at purchase** — "$140 feels like a leap" | Make the click feel low-commitment: easy-exit returns / reassurance that it's reversible | Decision | Subscription/commitment friction (Netflix easy cancellation) + support reassurance (Zappos free returns) — friction→solution |
| 5 | **"Habit of the present"** — comfort with the bedding they already own | Frame switching as a small, familiar, *better* step — name what they're giving up, show it's easy to start | Consideration → Decision | Anxiety / habit-of-the-present counter-move: familiar + better + low-friction transition (iPhone/Slack onboarding pattern) |
| 6 | **Can't touch the fabric before buying** | Home-try-on / sleep-on-it trial adapted to bedding | Consideration → Decision | Anticipating barriers (Warby Parker home try-on; Carvana 7-day return) — pre-empt the doubt before the cart |
| 7 | **No validation after purchase** — repeat stuck at 31% | Personalized post-purchase touch (care/styling note) that reinforces "you chose right" | Retention | Signpost example: personalized thank-you email w/ styling tips → satisfaction/validation |

**Timing note (context is everything):** the need spikes at a *moment* — move-in / renovation-complete week. A context-timed message ("Just moved in? Make the bedroom the first room that feels done.") lands the Awareness/Consideration signpost when the need is hottest. Pattern: state the timing trigger, then the offer (the HelloFresh "too busy to cook this week?" / "New Year, New You" structure). "Sensitive timing is everything."

**One next action:** Approve rows 1–3 (the Decision-stage trust/relief + checkout fixes — that's where your leak and your valuation lift are). Tell me your current return/guarantee terms and I'll draft the exact on-PDP guarantee + trial copy, then regenerate the doc so it's drop-in for the diligence pack.

### Assertions (oracle — machine-checkable)

- [tool-call] `export_strategy_doc` invoked with `{avatar_id:"av_B0HOM0003", section:"customer_journey_map", format:"doc"}`; returned the 5-stage signpost map + the risk/friction fix list; only these 2 tools called.
- [schema] `export_strategy_doc` output contains both a per-stage signpost map (5 stages, each with its signpost emotion + gap) and a fix list whose rows carry {fear, device, stage, book-pattern}; idempotent per avatar snapshot.
- [skill-faithful] "Customers never buy randomly — every move is fueled by an emotional signpost; a leaking stage = an unaddressed signpost" traces to `01-the-five-stages-and-signposts/00-the-five-stage-funnel.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The five stages and their signpost emotions — Awareness=curiosity/frustration, Consideration=hope/anticipation, Decision=trust/relief (+ "Shopper High"), Retention=satisfaction/validation, Advocacy=pride/belonging — plus the example moves (real-people imagery/warm tone; testimonials + clear refund policy; personalized thank-you w/ styling tips; unprompted reviews/unboxing) all trace to `01-the-five-stages-and-signposts/02-the-signposts-per-stage.md`.
- [skill-faithful] "Identify the dominant category fear, then pick the risk-removal category that disarms it; state the guarantee plainly at Decision" + the named devices (Casper risk-free trial, Zappos returns, Chewy satisfaction guarantee) trace to `02-removing-anxiety-and-risk/01-risk-removal-strategies.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The "habit of the present" (Moesta JTBD) as the top adoption barrier + the familiar-and-better, low-friction counter-move trace to `02-removing-anxiety-and-risk/00-overcoming-anxiety-habit-of-the-present.md`; "reassurance lowers perceived risk" framing traces to `02-removing-anxiety-and-risk/02-risk-mitigation.md`.
- [skill-faithful] Checkout/commitment friction fixes (Amazon one-click, Google autofill/Apple Pay, Netflix easy cancellation, Zappos free returns) + "every friction removed is conversion recovered" trace to `03-timing-and-friction/03-addressing-friction-points.md`; the see-it-through-their-eyes home-try-on / 7-day-return barriers (Warby Parker, Carvana) trace to `03-timing-and-friction/04-anticipating-barriers.md`.
- [skill-faithful] "Sensitive timing is everything" / context-fit framing traces to `03-timing-and-friction/00-context-and-timing.md`; the "state the timing trigger, then the offer" pattern + the move-in-week message trace to `03-timing-and-friction/01-timing-related-messages.md`.
- [persona-adapt:P1] Compression + done-for-you: coach led with the answer + one-line why, used HER data (ATC-soft, repeat 31%, $140 AOV), skipped definitions she knows ("don't generic-advice me on trust"), PICKED the right risk devices instead of dumping the 6-category/10-example menus, filled the map + fix-list tables FOR her, batched into two tables, and ended with exactly one next action. ≤1 context-extraction request; no blank worksheet returned.
- [artifact] A complete customer journey map (5-stage signpost map) + a shovel-ready risk-removal/friction fix list (7 rows: fear → device → stage → book pattern) + a timing note is present and edit-ready.
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; no invented reviews/valuation/canon — IV-OS `log_asset` shown as an `⟦iv-os:⟧` mock, never invented as real data; all numbers (ATC-soft, 31% repeat, $140 AOV) are Maya's own supplied data, no fabricated stats or book facts beyond the cited Ch8 skills.
